methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Rune Technologies is a Series A-stage Defense & Security company founded by David Tuttle and Peter Goldsborough that has raised $20M+. The peer universe consists of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $20M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $40M. Peer-Median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M. The median of this peer set is $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $34M to $345M, with a median of $170M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around comparable selection and stage-specific multiples in the defense sector. The lower bound ($34M) emerges from conservative stage-progression logic; the peer-median method introduces substantially higher values given Rune's positioning relative to funded defense-tech peers. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the peer set, revenue traction not captured in the raised-capital proxy, and sector-specific risk factors. Further diligence on customer pipeline and IP defensibility would narrow this range.
